headline: Waste Services Expertise Aids WM Amid High Debt & Low Liquidity
summary: WM rides on strong infrastructure and acquisitions for steady growth, but high debt, liquidity strain and modest momentum may temper investor enthusiasm.

headline: Had You Invested $1K in Waste Management or Republic Services 10 Years Ago, Here’s What You’d Have Now
summary: Waste Management (NYSE: WM) and Republic Services (NYSE: RSG) are not glamorous. They pick up trash. But over the past decade, both companies quietly compounded shareholder wealth by leaning into pricing power, long-term municipal contracts, and a growing sustainability angle through renewable natural gas and recycling automation. Waste Management expanded its footprint dramatically with its ... Had You Invested $1K in Waste Management or Republic Services 10 Years Ago, Here’s What You’d Have No

headline: How Investors May Respond To Waste Management (WM) Expanding Into Large-Scale Renewable Natural Gas Production
summary: In recent months, Waste Management has continued to expand beyond traditional trash collection by investing billions of dollars into 20 new renewable natural gas plants across North America, reinforcing its role in both waste services and energy production. This push into renewable natural gas, supported by difficult-to-replicate landfill assets and exposure to ESG and carbon credit markets, is reshaping how investors think about the company’s long-term business mix. We’ll now examine how...

headline: Waste Management Expands Into Renewable Gas As Valuation Near Fair Value
summary: Waste Management is committing several billion dollars to build 20 new renewable natural gas plants across its landfill network. The move signals a shift toward producing low carbon energy from landfill gas, alongside its traditional waste collection and disposal services. This build out is intended to tap into ESG driven demand and participate in carbon credit markets tied to cleaner fuel production. For investors tracking NYSE:WM, this push into renewable natural gas comes on top of a...
